curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.subtopic: arithmetic and geometric sequences; nth term rule
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Recognise and describe arithmetic and geometric sequences, generate terms, and form/use an nth-term rule to predict any term and compare growth patterns. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

verse_ality.driving_question: How can we spot, describe, and predict patterns in numbers—and what do different patterns tell us about growth?

knowing_world.focus: Learn to recognise arithmetic sequences (constant difference) and geometric sequences (constant multiplier), and use an nth-term rule to describe and predict any term in a sequence.
key_concepts: Arithmetic sequences: increase/decrease by a constant value (common difference)
Geometric sequences: multiply/divide by a constant value (common ratio)
nth-term rule (e.g., a_n = 3n + 1) for predicting any term
Using a term-to-term rule to continue a sequence

human_story.focus: Humans have always noticed patterns—in nature, architecture, music, and time. Mathematicians from Babylon to India explored sequences long before calculators, showing how pattern-spotting is a powerful human skill.

expressive_self.focus: Use creativity to create, represent, and communicate number patterns.
activities: Create an abstract artwork using number patterns—colour by multiples or powers.
Write a short story where a hidden pattern helps solve a mystery.
Make a sequence playlist: each song must follow a pattern in duration or title numbers!

designed_world.focus: Use an interactive tool to explore, extend, and analyse different types of sequences, including patterns that are not purely arithmetic or geometric.
design_challenges: How do you know if a sequence is arithmetic or geometric?
Can you find a pattern that is neither?
What happens when you reverse or alternate a pattern?
Which sequences grow faster and why?
Create your own “mystery pattern” and test whether others can identify the rule.

regulation_supports: Clap, step, or drum a pattern out loud; alternate fast and slow beats and challenge a partner to guess the rule.

pathways.name: Final Task - Mystery Pattern Challenge
pathways.focus: Design your own “Mystery Pattern” and challenge a classmate to continue it and describe the rule.
products: A sequence you created (at least 8 terms), with a clear term-to-term rule or nth-term rule
A short set of instructions/questions for a classmate (e.g., “find the next 3 terms”, “find the 10th term”)
An answer key explaining the rule and showing how to find a later term
assessment_criteria: Conceptual Understanding — Explains the difference between arithmetic (linear) and geometric growth
Fluency & Accuracy — Finds later terms (e.g., 10th term) quickly and correctly
Application — Describes where patterns like these appear in real life
Independence & Reflection — Identifies which type of sequence was trickiest and why

required: Can spot simple patterns and describe a rule in words
Basic algebra awareness that n can represent the term number

builds_toward: Linear sequences and graphs (linking nth term to y = mx + c)
